grounding_note: Expansion batch: more proceedings-status and cross-system disclosures. Safety-aware.

scenarios.opening_query: My fiancée has been waiting abroad for two years and the consulate keeps asking for the same documents.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: International Law > General Immigration/Visas || Administrative Law > General (Federal)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The petition is actually approved — the case is stuck in a State Department 'administrative processing' black hole, and I'm told the remedy now is a federal mandamus-style delay action.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The petition's approved — it's frozen in State Department 'administrative processing,' and the remedy now is a federal delay action.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the case is an approved petition stuck in agency delay
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The petition itself was returned with a request for more relationship evidence — the filing needs to be rebuilt properly.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The petition came back wanting more relationship evidence — it needs rebuilding.
scenarios.vetting_note: Delay-litigation vs petition-repair; approved-but-stuck is a distinct posture users can state.

scenarios.opening_query: My husband was detained at his regular immigration check-in this morning and I don't know where he is.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: International Law > Deportation || Criminal Law > Post Conviction/Appeals
scenarios.hidden_fact: The detention trigger is his 2011 plea — his old lawyer swore it was 'immigration safe,' and the deportation defense lawyer we called says vacating that plea is the only real path.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's his 2011 plea — the old lawyer called it 'immigration safe,' and the removal-defense lawyer says vacating it is the only real path.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether attacking an old conviction is the operative remedy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No conviction involved — it's his expired work permit and a missed renewal; he needs bond and removal defense now.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No conviction — it's the expired work permit and missed renewal. He needs bond and removal defense.
scenarios.vetting_note: Crimmigration fork under detention urgency; both variants realistic.

scenarios.opening_query: I've had my green card for nine years and want to finally become a citizen, but I'm nervous about my record.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: International Law > General Immigration/Visas || Criminal Law > Expunction
scenarios.hidden_fact: The immigration consult said my two old misdemeanors don't block naturalization — what I actually need first is the state-court record cleanup so the certified dispositions exist to file with the N-400.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The consult says the old misdemeanors don't block citizenship — what I need first is state-court record cleanup so certified dispositions exist for the N-400.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether state record work precedes the immigration filing
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: My record's actually clean — the nervousness is about a long trip abroad that may have broken continuous residence; that's the citizenship question.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: My record's clean — it's a long trip abroad that may have broken continuous residence.
scenarios.vetting_note: Sequencing disclosure between state record relief and naturalization practice.

scenarios.opening_query: My employee's work authorization is expiring and HR says we have to let her go next month.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: International Law > General Immigration/Visas || Labor & Employment > Discrimination - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: Her renewal receipt actually auto-extends her authorization for months — HR refuses to accept it and singled her out; she's asked me about her rights, and it smells like national-origin discrimination.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Her renewal receipt auto-extends the authorization — HR won't accept it and singled her out. She's asking about her rights; it looks like discrimination.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether refusal to honor valid work-authorization documents is discriminatory
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: There's no receipt — her renewal never got filed, and the question is whether an emergency filing can save her status.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: There's no receipt — the renewal never got filed, and it's about an emergency filing now.
scenarios.vetting_note: Document-abuse by employers is an employment claim; filing-gap is immigration work.

scenarios.opening_query: We want to hire a brilliant engineer from abroad but the process defeats me every time I read about it.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: International Law > General Immigration/Visas || International Law > Business And Corporate
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's bigger than one visa — we're opening a subsidiary in her country and structuring a cross-border employment and IP arrangement; the corporate structure question comes first.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's more than a visa — we're opening a subsidiary in her country with cross-border employment and IP arrangements. Structure comes first.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether cross-border corporate structuring is the primary need
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No foreign entity plans — we just need the right work-visa petition done correctly this cycle.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No foreign entity — just the right work-visa petition, done right, this cycle.
scenarios.vetting_note: Visa-vs-structure scope disclosure on the employer side.

scenarios.opening_query: They denied my asylum case and my lawyer disappeared on me. The clock is ticking.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: International Law > Deportation || General Litigation > Malpractice (Legal)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The denial happened because he missed the filing deadline for my evidence and never told me — the appeal I need may rest on proving his ineffective assistance, and I also want him held accountable.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He missed my evidence deadline and hid it — the appeal may rest on his ineffective assistance, and I want him accountable too.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether prior counsel's proven error is central to the remedy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: My lawyer did fine — the judge just didn't believe the country-conditions evidence; I need the appeal argued.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He did fine — the judge didn't credit the country evidence. I need the appeal argued.
scenarios.vetting_note: Ineffective-assistance overlays malpractice on removal defense; users hold the deadline fact.
